claim: Mice defective for deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) polymerase I (polI) reveal increased sensitivity to ionizing radiation (IR).

result.claim: Mice defective for deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) polymerase I (polI) reveal increased sensitivity to ionizing radiation (IR).
result.reasoning: The initial searches for similar propositions and chunks did not yield any direct evidence supporting or refuting the claim that mice defective for DNA polymerase I (polI) reveal increased sensitivity to ionizing radiation (IR). The search results contained propositions and chunks related to other genes, general DNA damage responses, or different types of polymerases (e.g., pol mu, pol lambda), but nothing specifically linking DNA polymerase I deficiency to ionizing radiation sensitivity in mice. Therefore, based on the current knowledge base, there is insufficient evidence to verify the claim.

evidence.text: Rpl38 mutant mice exhibited tissue-specific patterning defects.
evidence.source: Ribosome-Mediated Specificity in Hox mRNA Translation and Vertebrate Tissue Patterning

evidence.text: The gp130F759/F759 mice showed an enhanced acute phase reaction.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: Perturbation of mesenchymal cells in a mouse model of Shwachman-Diamond syndrome induces activation of DNA damage responses in hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells.
evidence.source: Mesenchymal Inflammation Drives Genotoxic Stress in Hematopoietic Stem Cells and Predicts Disease Evolution in Human Pre-leukemia.

evidence.text: The mutations result in enhanced suppression of p53 in response to ionizing radiation exposure.
evidence.source: Mosaic PPM1D mutations are associated with predisposition to breast and ovarian cancer

evidence.text: Homozygous mutant mice displayed unchanged OFF responses to light.
evidence.source: Specific deficit of the ON response in visual transmission by targeted disruption of the mGIuR6 gene
Lethal autoimmune myocarditis in interferon-gamma receptor-deficient mice: enhanced disease severity by impaired inducible nitric oxide synthase induction.

evidence.text: IFN-gamma receptor-deficient mice (IFN-gammaR(-/-)) on a BALB/c background immunized with a peptide derived from cardiac alpha-myosin heavy chain develop severe myocarditis.
evidence.source: Lethal autoimmune myocarditis in interferon-gamma receptor-deficient mice: enhanced disease severity by impaired inducible nitric oxide synthase induction.

evidence.text: Homozygous mutant mice showed a loss of ON responses to light.
evidence.source: Specific deficit of the ON response in visual transmission by targeted disruption of the mGIuR6 gene

evidence.text: Mice deficient in Irp2 were protected from cigarette smoke (CS)-induced experimental COPD.
evidence.source: Mitochondrial iron chelation ameliorates cigarette-smoke induced bronchitis and emphysema in mice

evidence.text: Th1-type cytokine production was increased in the gp130F759/F759 mice.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.
